title: 10 best herbal creams for cirrhosis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with cirrhosis because of its potent antioxidant properties, which aid in protecting liver cells from damage caused by free radicals. The cream's active compound, silymarin, has been shown to improve liver function and reduce inflammation, allowing the liver to regenerate and repair damaged tissue. Additionally, milk thistle creams may help to stabilize the liver's membrane, preventing further damage and promoting overall liver health. By using a topical application, the benefits of milk thistle can be easily absorbed into the skin, providing targeted relief for cirrhosis sufferers.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: A flavonoid complex found in milk thistle, silymarin helps protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals and toxins, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis.

Phenolic acids: These compounds in milk thistle cream have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce liver fibrosis and improve liver function in individuals with cirrhosis.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid content of milk thistle cream has been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing in the liver tissue affected by cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Milk thistle roots have been traditionally used to treat liver and digestive issues, making them an effective ingredient in creams for cirrhosis.
Seeds: The seeds of milk thistle contain silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that helps protect the liver from damage and promotes its regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried milk thistle seeds silybum marianum in an amount of 100 grams for the preparation.
Grind the dried milk thistle seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix one teaspoon of powdered milk thistle seeds with two tablespoons of sweet almond oil and four tablespoons of coconut oil in a small bowl for 10 minutes.

Heat the mixture gently over low heat in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second intervals, stirring between each interval, until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f for 15 minutes.

Pour the warm mixture into sterilized glass jars with tight-fitting lids and let it cool before storing them in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 40°f for up to six months or until used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis, thereby slowing the progression of cirrhosis. Additionally, dandelion's bitter principles can stimulate bile production, which can aid in the elimination of toxins from the body, further supporting liver health and potentially reversing some of the damage caused by cirrhosis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion cream's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in liver cells, which can contribute to liver damage and scarring associated with cirrhosis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion cream may help alleviate inflammation and swelling in the liver and surrounding tissues, which is often a hallmark of cirrhosis.

Choleretic and cholekinetic effects: Dandelion cream's ability to stimulate bile production and flow (choleresis) and improve bile duct contraction and relaxation (cholekinesis) can help reduce liver damage by improving the elimination of toxic substances and reducing the accumulation of bilirubin in the bloodstream, a common complication of cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing liver damage and inflammation associated with cirrhosis.
Rhyzomes: Containing saponins, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and reduce liver fibrosis, a common complication of cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion leaves and flowers according to botanical guidelines for quality control weighing 100g.
Dry the plant material in a temperature-controlled environment at 30°c for 24 hours followed by storage in an airtight container.
Combine dried taraxacum officinale with carrier oils such as sweet almond oil and coconut oil in a ratio of 1:4 for 48 hour maceration process.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids after 2 hours preparation time.
Mix the filtered herbal extract with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a ratio of 3:1 at room temperature for 30 minutes before filling and sealing in sterilized containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain potent antioxidants and flavonoids that help to repair and protect damaged liver cells. The creams' anti-inflammatory properties also reduce liver scarring and fibrosis, promoting a healthier liver environment. Additionally, the creams may help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the liver, allowing it to function more efficiently and effectively. By addressing these underlying issues, maidenhair tree creams can provide relief from cirrhosis symptoms and promote overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in Maidenhair fern may help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to skin conditions like dermatitis and itchiness often associated with cirrhosis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Adiantum spp. contains flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which may help reduce inflammation in the liver and skin tissues, alleviating symptoms of cirrhosis such as pain, itching, and fatigue.

Hepatoprotective properties: Some studies suggest that Adiantum spp. extracts may possess hepatoprotective effects by reducing oxidative stress and inflammatory responses in liver cells, which could potentially help alleviate liver damage caused by cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to treat liver inflammation and scarring, the roots are rich in saponins and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Barks: The barks contain tannins and phenolic acids that help protect the liver from damage and reduce inflammation, making them effective for treating cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them for at least two weeks in the shade away from direct sunlight.
Grind 200g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Melt 100g of coconut oil and 50g of beeswax together in a double boiler until they form a smooth consistent mixture taking approximately 20 minutes.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture stirring well to combine adding 10 drops of lavender essential oil for its calming properties.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars filling them to the top and allowing it to cool and harden at room temperature taking approximately one hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with cirrhosis because of their potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredients, including boswellic acids and terpenes, work to reduce inflammation and scarring in the liver, thereby alleviating symptoms such as pain, swelling, and fatigue. Additionally, frankincense has been shown to promote collagen synthesis, which can help improve liver tissue damage and overall liver function. This natural therapy may provide a safe and effective alternative for managing cirrhosis-related complications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense has been shown to reduce inflammation, which is a common symptom and contributor to cirrhosis, helping to alleviate symptoms such as pain, swelling, and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Frankincense's antioxidant activity helps protect the liver from further damage caused by oxidative stress and free radicals, which can accelerate cirrhosis progression and organ failure.

Immune-modulating properties: Frankincense has been found to stimulate the immune system, helping to fight off infections that can exacerbate cirrhosis, such as viral hepatitis, and promoting overall liver health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the frankincense plant contain bioactive compounds like boswellic acids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce liver inflammation and scarring associated with cirrhosis.
Barks: Frankincense barks are rich in oleo-gum resin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that can help alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis such as abdominal pain, bloating, and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 tablespoons of beeswax pellets and 4 tablespoons of coconut oil into a double boiler setup.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried boswellia serrata powder to the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture stir well to combine.
Allow the mixture to cool slightly then add in 10 drops of frankincense essential oil and 5 drops of lavender essential oil.
Continue stirring until the mixture reaches a smooth consistency then transfer it to an sterilized glass jar store it in a cool dry place.
Apply a small amount of the cream topically to affected areas twice daily as needed for up to 8 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis associated with the disease. Curcumin also exhibits antioxidant properties, which help neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress in the liver cells. Additionally, turmeric creams may improve liver function by promoting the body's natural detoxification processes and reducing inflammation in the liver tissue, ultimately helping to slow down or even reverse cirrhosis-related damage.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Turmeric creams contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds like curcumin, which helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, slowing down the progression of cirrhosis.
Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of further liver damage and scarring associated with cirrhosis.
Immunomodulatory effects: Turmeric creams may also modulate the immune response in the liver, helping to reduce inflammation and fibrosis (scarring) that can exacerbate cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis.
Rhyzomes: Contains high concentrations of volatile oils, which aid in reducing liver fibrosis and improving liver function.
remedy_recipe: Extract turmeric powder from fresh rhizomes of curcuma longa for 2 hours using 300ml water and 150g powder ratio.
Combine the extracted turmeric liquid with 100g beeswax and 50g coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Add 10% vitamin e oil, 5% glycerin, and 1% triethanolamine to the mixture while stirring continuously for 15 minutes.
Cool the mixture to room temperature then whip it using an electric mixer for 10 minutes until it forms a smooth cream consistency.
Transfer the curcuma longa cream into sterilized tubes or jars and store in the refrigerator at a temperature below 40°f for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce liver damage caused by chronic inflammation. The herbs in these creams, such as turmeric and ginger, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to the progression of cirrhosis. By reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, herbal tea creams may slow down or even reverse fibrosis, improving overall liver function and quality of life for individuals with cirrhosis.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: A flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, silymarin helps protect liver cells from damage and inflammation, which can reduce the severity of cirrhosis symptoms such as fatigue, abdominal pain, and jaundice.

Vitamin K: Some herbal tea creams contain vitamin K-rich herbs like ginkgo biloba or chamomile, which help regulate blood clotting and may slow the progression of liver fibrosis by reducing scarring and inflammation in the liver tissue.

Antioxidants: Herbal teas rich in antioxidants, such as green tea, turmeric, or ginger, can help reduce oxidative stress and damage to liver cells caused by cirrhosis, which may slow down the progression of liver disease and improve overall health outcomes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, roots help reduce inflammation and improve liver function.
Barks: Containing tannins and phenolic acids, barks have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective against cirrhosis-related liver damage.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality camellia sinensis leaves and dry them thoroughly using a food dehydrator for at least 24 hours.
Combine one cup of dried camellia sinensis leaves with two cups of distilled water in a large saucepan and bring to a boil for 10 minutes.
Decoct the mixture by reducing heat and simmering for an additional 30 minutes, then strain through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Mix one tablespoon of beeswax pellets and one teaspoon of coconut oil with the camellia sinensis extract and heat gently in a double boiler until melted and well combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow to cool and set at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and scarring associated with cirrhosis. By reducing oxidative stress and promoting the body's natural detoxification processes, ginger creams may help slow down or even reverse the progression of cirrhosis. Additionally, ginger has been found to improve digestion and liver function, further supporting its potential benefits in managing this condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal ginger creams containing antioxidants like gingerol and shogaol help neutralize free radicals in the body, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with cirrhosis.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and paradols, alleviate liver inflammation and reduce the risk of fibrosis and scarring caused by cirrhosis.

Gastrointestinal soothing properties: Herbal ginger creams can help soothe gastrointestinal discomforts like nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain often experienced by patients with cirrhosis, allowing for improved digestion and nutrient absorption.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help alleviate liver inflammation and improve liver function.
Rhyzomes: Contains high levels of antioxidants and bioactive compounds that protect the liver from damage and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality zingiber officinale roots and clean them thoroughly with warm water.
Dry the cleaned zingiber officinale roots in shade for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind dried zingiber officinale roots into fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of powdered zingiber officinale with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with cirrhosis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The gel from the aloe plant has been shown to soothe and calm irritated liver tissue, reducing inflammation and damage caused by the disease. Additionally, aloe's ability to stimulate collagen production can help restore damaged liver cells and improve liver function. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, herbal aloe creams may slow or even reverse cirrhosis, improving overall health and quality of life for those affected by this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation in the liver, which is a common symptom of cirrhosis.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in aloe vera, including vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and other polyphenols, neutralize free radicals that can damage liver cells and contribute to the progression of cirrhosis.

Wound-healing and tissue regeneration properties: Aloe vera's gel has been shown to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and enhancing tissue regeneration, which can help reduce scarring and improve liver function in individuals with cirrhosis.

remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including pure aloe vera gel, coconut oil, beeswax, vitamin e oil, and frankincense essential oil.
Weigh 200 grams of aloe vera gel and mix it with 100 grams of coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Melt 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler and slowly add the mixture to the aloe vera gel mixture while stirring continuously.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes before whipping until it reaches a creamy consistency.
Add 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil and 2 drops of frankincense essential oil and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Purple coneflower contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce liver inflammation and damage associated with cirrhosis.

Antioxidant activity: The cream's antioxidant properties, derived from flavonoids and polyphenols, help protect the liver cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing tissue degeneration and promoting liver health.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried echinacea purpurea flowers at the peak of their potency and store them in airtight containers for later use.
Combine one part echinacea purpurea flowers with two parts coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and preserving the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax pellets at a ratio of one part oil to one part wax, melting in a double boiler and stirring until fully incorporated.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars, allowing it to cool and solidify before storing in the refrigerator for up to six months.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with cirrhosis because it contains echinacea, a natural anti-inflammatory that reduces liver inflammation and scarring. The cream also has antioxidant properties that protect the liver from further damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, echinacea has been shown to improve liver function and regeneration, promoting healthy liver tissue growth. By addressing inflammation and oxidative stress, purple coneflower creams may help alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis, such as fatigue, abdominal pain, and digestive issues.

Stinging nettle creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that help soothe and protect damaged liver tissue. The cream's ability to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress may slow down the progression of fibrosis, a hallmark of cirrhosis. Additionally, stinging nettle's natural anticoagulant properties may help prevent blood clotting in the liver, further reducing scarring and promoting healthy liver function. Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle creams can help protect the liver from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to liver disease and progression of cirrhosis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers and dry them for 2 hours in the shade.
Grind 50 grams of dried urtica dioica into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered herb with 20 grams of beeswax, 15 grams of coconut oil, and 5 grams of vitamin e oil for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to melt the wax and oils.
Pour the cream into glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature below 8°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. This can help to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis associated with cirrhosis, ultimately slowing down the progression of the disease. Flavonoids: A class of plant compounds present in licorice extracts that have been shown to possess anti-fibrotic activity, which may help prevent or slow down the progression of fibrosis (scarring) in the liver due to cirrhosis.

Triterpenoids: A type of saponin found in licorice root that has been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and chemokines, which can contribute to liver inflammation and damage in cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate liver damage associated with cirrhosis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and sift it through a fine mesh sieve.
Combine the sifting residue with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of coconut oil in a glass mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture at a low temperature of 45 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to infuse the oils with the herbal properties.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding any solids and let it cool to room temperature.
Whip 100 grams of beeswax with 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil at high speed for 5 minutes then blend it with the cooled herbal oil mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain a unique combination of saponins, mucilages, and flavonoids that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver. These bioactive compounds help to soothe and protect the liver cells from damage caused by chronic liver disease, promoting regeneration and repair. Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the liver caused by cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality marshmallow root althaea officinalis in summer months for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm and well-ventilated area for at least seven days.
Grind 30 grams of dried marshmallow root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered marshmallow root with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a warm water bath for 20-25 minutes stirring occasionally until the mixture thickens.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa flowers at dawn on a sunny day and rinse them thoroughly.
Combine 100g of dried rosa rugosa flowers with 500ml of olive oil in a heat-resistant glass jar.
Steam the mixture for 30 minutes, then filter it through cheesecloth into another clean container.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil with the filtered herbal infusion in a double boiler until smooth.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation in the liver, which is a common symptom of the condition. remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry lavandula angustifolia flowers to obtain high-quality dried buds for infusion.
Combine 50 grams of dried lavender buds with 500 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion and prevent sedimentation.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining 300 milliliters of the infused oil.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax with 200 milliliters of the infused oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture to 160°f for 10 minutes to create a smooth and consistent cream base.

Whip the cooled mixture using an electric mixer on low speed until it reaches a creamy consistency requiring about 10 minutes.

Filter the resulting oil infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding any solids after 20 minutes of pressing.
Mix the filtered oil infusion with 10 grams of beeswax and heat the blend in a double boiler until the wax is fully incorporated and melted within 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams helps with cirrhosis because of its rich antioxidant properties, which help to combat oxidative stress in the liver. The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce scarring and fibrosis, promoting healthy tissue regeneration. Additionally, field horsetail's silicates help to stabilize the structure of liver cells, preventing further damage from occurring. By addressing these underlying issues, field horsetail creams can help to alleviate symptoms and slow down the progression of cirrhosis.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins present in Field Horsetail have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and scarring associated with Cirrhosis.

Silica: Silica, a key component of Field Horsetail, has been found to improve liver function and regenerate damaged liver tissue, helping to alleviate symptoms of Cirrhosis such as jaundice and abdominal pain.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in Field Horsetail help protect the liver from further damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the progression of Cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain high levels of silica and antioxidants that help soothe and regenerate liver tissue damaged by cirrhosis.
Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, reducing liver inflammation associated with cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried equisetum arvense leaves and stems in the morning for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material using shade or low-temperature drying methods for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered equisetum arvense with coconut oil at a ratio of 1:2 by weight in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and fill into sterilized jars after it has cooled to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain a unique combination of herbs that have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to promote liver health. The creams' active ingredients, such as peony root and licorice root, help to soothe inflammation and reduce oxidative stress in the liver cells, thereby improving liver function and reducing scarring associated with cirrhosis. Additionally, the creams' anti-inflammatory properties may also help to alleviate symptoms such as fatigue and abdominal pain often experienced by individuals with cirrhosis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chinese Peony extracts have been shown to reduce inflammation in the liver, which is a common symptom of cirrhosis, by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and free radical formation.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in Chinese Peony cream, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, can help protect the liver from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to cirrhosis progression.

Fibrosis-inhibiting: Chinese Peony has been found to inhibit the formation of excessive collagen fibers in the liver, which is a hallmark feature of fibrotic diseases like cirrhosis, potentially slowing down or even reversing liver scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to treat liver fibrosis and inflammation, roots of Chinese Peony are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are employed to reduce liver damage and improve liver function, as they contain compounds that inhibit liver fibrosis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried paeonia lactiflora roots and 20g of sweet almond oil for the extraction process.
Combine 10g of the dried paeonia lactiflora roots with 200ml of distilled water in a heat-proof container.
Boil the mixture at 100°c for 30 minutes then steep it for an additional 60 minutes to create a strong extract.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and mix 10g of the resulting extract with 50g of beeswax and 20g of sweet almond oil.
Heat the mixture at 70°c for 15 minutes while stirring occasionally until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with cirrhosis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce liver inflammation and scarring. The cream's active compound, berberine, has been shown to improve liver function and reduce damage caused by chronic liver disease. Additionally, goldenseal's antimicrobial properties may help to combat secondary bacterial infections that can exacerbate cirrhosis symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, goldenseal creams may help alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis, such as fatigue, digestive issues, and jaundice.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key constituent of Goldenseal, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce liver damage and inflammation associated with cirrhosis.

Sulfur-containing compounds: The sulfur-rich compounds in Goldenseal, such as berberastin and berbamin, may help protect the liver from damage caused by toxins and oxidative stress, thereby supporting liver health and potentially slowing disease progression.

Alkaloids: The alkaloid content of Goldenseal, including berberine and others, has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help combat infections that can exacerbate cirrhosis symptoms and reduce the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of goldenseal contain berberine, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, making it effective in treating cirrhosis.
Rhyzomes: Goldenseal rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat liver and digestive issues, including cirrhosis, due to their ability to stimulate bile production and improve liver function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried hydrastis canadensis root bark and grind it into a fine powder.
Combine 10 grams of the powdered root bark with 20 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, allowing the oil to absorb the herbal properties.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool before straining the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil to the liquid extract, stirring until fully incorporated.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for cirrhosis are natural topical treatments that utilize the medicinal properties of plants to alleviate symptoms associated with liver damage. These creams help by reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and improving skin integrity, which is often compromised in individuals with cirrhosis. Examples include creams containing aloe vera, chamomile, and witch hazel, which have been shown to reduce itching and inflammation. By using these herbal creams, individuals with cirrhosis can experience improved overall quality of life, reduced discomfort, and enhanced wound healing, ultimately improving their daily functioning and emotional well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat cirrhosis with herbal creams is by using a combination of herbs that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Turmeric cream, containing curcumin, has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and scarring. Milk thistle cream, rich in silymarin, helps protect the liver from further damage. Apply these creams topically to the affected area, following the recommended dosage and guidelines.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cirrhosis that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions such as psoriasis, eczema, and acne. These conditions share similarities with cirrhosis in terms of inflammation and scarring. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and turmeric have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching associated with these conditions.
